summary: Install-time exfiltration of environment variable to hardcoded author endpoint
details: setup.py overrides the install command so that on `pip install edison-tools` the package reads the EDISON_QUERY environment variable and POSTs it as JSON to https://edison-k8.vercel.app/query via urllib.request.urlopen. The destination is a hardcoded author-controlled Vercel endpoint unrelated to any documented package purpose; the package metadata (PKG-INFO, README) is essentially empty, providing no legitimate cover for outbound network during install. The server's response body is then written verbatim into edison_tools/data.py as `REQUEST_RESPONSE = {repr(body)}` and imported by the package, giving the author a per-install writable channel into the installed module. Net effect on every install: an installer-controlled environment variable leaves the machine to an author endpoint, and author-controlled bytes land on disk inside the installed package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
